data.body:
PROGRESS — slice 3/4 done (config-loop, hourly /loop iteration). jira.* (url/user/project) now routes through the typed Config as the config-FILE layer; secret JIRA_API_TOKEN stays env-ONLY.
- config.py: added the jira.* canonical env names to _CANONICAL_ENV_NAMES (jira.url<-JIRA_URL, jira.user<-JIRA_USER, jira.project<-JIRA_PROJECT, the Atlassian-standard unprefixed names; both env_overrides() and _env_signature() already consult _canonical_env_name so resolution + cache key agree).
- New shared resolver acli_subprocess.resolve_jira_settings(project_default="") -> JiraSettings(url, user, project, api_token): url/user/project from load_config().jira.* (env overrides file), api_token from os.environ ONLY (never a config key). On ConfigError it degrades to env-only so a malformed config never breaks a reconcile pass. project_default substitutes for an empty project (e.g. "DIG").
- Rerouted all 9 reconciler consumer sites: acli.transition_issue, acli_cli_ops.update_priority, fetcher (snapshot client + parent-map project key), applier (inbound write-back client + outbound CREATE client, project_default="DIG"), reconcile (comment client), _attestation.build_acli_client_from_env (project_default="DIG", required-check now on resolved url/user + env token), inbound_probe._resolve_env (required-check on resolved values).
- IMPORTANT test-fake fix: the resolver is read from the STABLE acli_subprocess floor, NOT the acli module returned by _load_acli() (tests swap that with a fake AcliClient-only module). Initially routing via acli_mod.resolve_jira_settings caused 60 failures; fixed by importing acli_subprocess at each site. The faked AcliClient still gets the same (empty) url/user as before, so behavior is preserved.
- Tests: new tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/state/test_config_jira_wiring.py (11 cases: param x location {pyproject, rebar.toml, legacy dotted conf, XDG user, canonical env JIRA_URL, rebar -c} x precedence CLI>env>project>user>default + project_default + secret-token-env-only + malformed->env fallback + provenance + the _attestation required-check consumer). Updated test_inbound_probe message assertion. Fixed test_config_show's autouse env-cleaner to clear CANONICAL env names + legacy aliases (an exported ambient JIRA_URL/JIRA_USER was leaking into the 'sources' provenance).
- Subagent review CLEAN on all 8 invariants (secret env-only, canonical env mapping + cache key, test-fake robustness with no remaining buggy instance, required-check preserved, project_default preserved, precedence, no unused imports/stray reads, coverage). One MINOR out-of-scope finding: bridge_fsck.py (_engine_support, NOT the reconciler, not on any live path) has a 10th AcliClient site still reading JIRA_* env directly -> filed follow-up 3b92-8ef3-661b-49b2 (discovered_from 0ac6).
- Full offline suite green; ruff check + format clean.
REMAINING: slice 4 (llm.* via [tool.rebar.llm] in LLMConfig.from_env, keeping the stdlib-only/optional-extra boundary), then docs/config.md update + close 0ac6 + close epic a621.
